_note: Synthetic, reproducible labeled set for the recall A/B benchmark. Placeholder content only.

corpus.title: Quarantine flaky integration tests
corpus.body: When an integration test fails intermittently, quarantine it with a skip marker and an open ticket rather than retrying in CI. Retries hide the flake and waste minutes. The quarantine list is reviewed every sprint.

corpus.title: Cache dependencies in CI by lockfile hash
corpus.body: Key the CI dependency cache on a hash of the lockfile, not the branch name. Branch-keyed caches go stale and cause 'works on my machine' drift. A lockfile-hash key makes cache hits correct and reproducible.

corpus.title: Use git bisect to find regressions
corpus.body: To find the commit that introduced a regression, use git bisect with a scripted test (git bisect run). Bisect is logarithmic, so even a thousand-commit range resolves in about ten steps.

corpus.title: Add healthchecks to compose services
corpus.body: Give every docker compose service a HEALTHCHECK and make dependents wait on service_healthy, not service_started. Without it, an app container races its database and fails on the first connection.

corpus.title: Claim queue rows with SKIP LOCKED
corpus.body: For a Postgres-backed work queue, claim rows with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED. It lets concurrent workers grab disjoint rows without blocking each other or double-processing.

corpus.title: Log structured key-value events
corpus.body: Emit logs as structured key-value events, not interpolated prose. Structured fields are queryable; a sentence like 'processed 18 items' is not. Reserve free text for the human-readable message field only.

corpus.title: Delete feature flags after rollout
corpus.body: A feature flag is debt the moment it reaches 100 percent rollout. Schedule the cleanup ticket when you create the flag, with a removal date, or the codebase accretes dead branches.

corpus.title: Make webhook handlers idempotent
corpus.body: Webhook deliveries can arrive more than once, so handlers must be idempotent: dedupe on the provider's event id and treat a repeat as a no-op. Assume at-least-once delivery, never exactly-once.

corpus.title: Store timestamps in UTC
corpus.body: Store and compute on timestamps in UTC; convert to local time only at the display edge. A field named created_at must hold UTC. Mixing local and UTC at a service boundary is a classic source of off-by-hours bugs.

corpus.title: Retry with exponential backoff and jitter
corpus.body: Retries against a flaky dependency need exponential backoff plus jitter. Fixed-interval retries from many clients synchronize into a thundering herd that keeps the dependency down.

corpus.title: Keep pull requests small
corpus.body: Small pull requests get faster, deeper reviews. Past roughly four hundred changed lines, reviewer defect-detection drops sharply and approvals become rubber stamps. Split large changes by seam.

corpus.title: Never log full environment dumps
corpus.body: Never log the full process environment for debugging: it leaks secrets into log aggregators that have a wider audience than the host. Log the specific variable you need, redacted if sensitive.

corpus.title: Retry transient network calls, not assertions
corpus.body: Retry transient NETWORK failures (timeouts, 503s) at the client. Do NOT retry a failed test assertion to make it pass: that masks a real defect. Retrying is for infrastructure flakiness, not logic flakiness.

corpus.title: Size the database connection pool
corpus.body: Size the database connection pool to the workload, not the max. Too many connections starve the database; too few serialize requests. Pool exhaustion looks like a hang, not an error, so add a checkout timeout.

corpus.title: Use factories for test data
corpus.body: Build test fixtures with factories that take overrides, not giant shared fixtures. Shared fixtures couple unrelated tests and make a single edit cascade into dozens of failures.

corpus.title: Sample high-volume logs
corpus.body: For a hot path that logs thousands of times a second, sample (for example one in a hundred) rather than logging every event. Full-fidelity logging on a hot path can cost more than the work it describes.

questions.q: How should I handle a test that fails only sometimes in CI?
What should the CI dependency cache key be based on?
How do I find which commit introduced a regression?
Why does my app container fail to reach the database on startup in compose?
How do multiple workers pull from a Postgres queue without double-processing?
Should logs be sentences or fields?
When should I remove a feature flag?
My webhook handler runs twice for one event, what do I do?
What timezone should created_at be stored in?
How should clients retry a flaky dependency without overwhelming it?
How big should a pull request be for a good review?
Is it ok to log the whole environment to debug a prod issue?
How do I stop CI cache from going stale across branches?
What prevents a thundering herd when many clients retry at once?
How do I keep intermittent tests from wasting CI minutes on retries?
My test passes on the second run but fails on the first. Should I just add a retry?
Requests to a downstream service intermittently time out under load. How should clients behave?
Our service hangs instead of erroring when traffic spikes; connections seem stuck. What knob is wrong?
A single change to shared setup broke twenty unrelated tests. What pattern avoids that?
Logging every request on our busiest endpoint is expensive. What's the standard fix?
Is it fine to retry a failing unit-test assertion until it goes green?
